manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: spider, periscope, titanium, laser, buzzer, geisha, spinach, cactus, ruse.
Your opponent's team words are: serenity, overcoat, dusk, toothpick, melon, campus, walrus, potassium.
Distractor words are: puck, ammo, cavalry, bayou, courtroom, luncheon, penguin.
The kill words are: bridegroom.

message.content:
.CLUE: navy
TARGETS: periscope, titanium, laser, buzzer
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of the team's words, avoid opponent, distractor, kill. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them.

Team words: spider, periscope, titanium, laser, buzzer... etc. Need a clue that relates multiple.

Maybe "metal" -> titanium (metal), laser (metal? lasers use metal? not strong). Spider? not metal. Buzzer? Could be metal. Could be "electric"? laser, buzzer, periscope? maybe.

We must avoid opponent words: serenity, etc.

We want a clue to cover many of our words. Could use "science" -> titanium, laser, periscope, spider (biological), cactus? maybe. But spider is biology, but okay.

We need a single-word clue; cannot be "science" which is part of "scien"? Not a listed word, okay.

But must be semantically related; "science" is fairly generic.

But we must ensure not ambiguous with opponent words like "campus" (science campus), "dusk" (science of twilight). Might cause guesser to pick opponents inadvertently. But the clue is just a hint.

We need to list the target words we intend the partner to guess.

Pick maybe "laser" is too specific, can't be clue because it's a team word.

Maybe "electric". Relates to laser (electric), buzzer (electric), spider (electric spider? e.g., electric spider toy), periscope (uses electronics), titanium? metal used in electric? Not strong.

Maybe "jungle". Relates to spider, cactus, geisha? No.

Maybe "stealth". Relates to periscope, laser, spider (stealthy), ruse (a stealthy deception). Titanium (stealth aircraft). That's many.

But need to avoid opponent word "dusk" maybe also stealth.

The clue can't be part of any word: "stealth" not part of any list, ok.

Targets: periscope, laser, spider, ruse, titanium? Possibly.

But is "stealth" semantically related to titanium? Yes, stealth aircraft often use titanium. Might be a stretch but okay.

However, we risk opponent "dusk" (stealth under dusk). But the clue is "stealth". They may guess dusk incorrectly? That would be a mistake.

We can try "camouflage". Relates to spider (camouflage), cactus (camouflage), ruse (deception), periscope (camouflage?), laser (camouflage? maybe not). Might be too vague.

Maybe "green". Relates to spinach, cactus, maybe spider (green spider), but also opponent "melon" (green). That could cause guess error.

Better choose clue that strongly points to a subset, maybe a pair.

Could clue "submarine" -> periscope (submarine), laser (submarine laser?), titanium (submarine hull), buzzer (sonar?), not good.

Maybe "Japanese". Relates to geisha, maybe laser (Japanese laser tech), titanium (Japanese industry), but also not too many.

What about "prank"? Relates to ruse, buzzer (prank buzzer), spider (prank spider), maybe.

We want a clue covering 3-4 words.

Maybe "metal". Covers titanium, laser (laser is metal?), buzzer (metal), spider (metal spider?), periscope (metal), not strong.

Maybe "detect". Relates to periscope (detect), laser (detect), buzzer (detect alarm), ruse (detect deception). Not strong.

Could clue "toy". Spider (toy spider), buzzer (toy buzzer). Could be safe.

But we need multi-word.

Let's think: which of our words share a common theme? "laser", "periscope", "titanium", "buzzer" all are used in "submarines"? Actually periscope definitely. Laser used for targeting. Titanium for hull. Buzzer maybe for alarm. Could clue "sub". But "sub" is short.

Clue "navy". Relates to periscope, titanium (navy ships), laser (naval lasers), buzzer (naval alarms). Might be okay. Opponent words "dusk", "overcoat", etc not navy. So likely safe.

But "navy" is also a color: could lead to guess "cactus"? Not.

I'll choose clue "navy". Targets: periscope, titanium, laser, buzzer.

Check that "navy" is not part of any listed words. None contain "navy". Good.

We must ensure none of the opponent words might be associated with navy? "walrus" is Arctic navy? but maybe.

But okay.

Thus produce:

CLUE: navy
TARGETS: periscope, titanium, laser, buzzer

Return exactly format
